decisions.title: Performance concerns identified for the dashboard project
decisions.detail: There are some concerns regarding the performance of the dashboard project.

evidence.quote: there are some concerns about performance

decisions.title: Consideration for using a better database
decisions.detail: It was mentioned that a better database should probably be used at some point for the project.

evidence.quote: Someone mentioned we should probably use a better database at some point.

decisions.title: UI aesthetic improvement required
decisions.detail: The user interface needs to be improved to look nicer.

evidence.quote: The UI needs to look nicer.

decisions.title: Current UI design is not satisfactory
decisions.detail: There was an agreement among attendees that the current UI design is not great.

evidence.quote: We all agreed the current design isn't great.

decisions.title: Project timelines are undecided
decisions.detail: A discussion about project timelines took place, but no firm decisions were made regarding them.

evidence.quote: There was a discussion about timelines but nothing was firmly decided.

decisions.title: Potential future integration with CRM system
decisions.detail: There might be a need to integrate with the CRM system eventually.

evidence.quote: We might need to integrate with the CRM system eventually

decisions.title: Uncertainty regarding CRM integration timing and method
decisions.detail: The specifics of when or how the potential CRM system integration would occur are currently unknown.

evidence.quote: we're not sure when or how.

decisions.title: Budget is a concern
decisions.detail: Budget was briefly mentioned and identified as a concern, though no specific numbers were shared.

evidence.quote: Budget was mentioned briefly — it's a concern but no numbers were shared.

decisions.title: Requirement for more thorough testing
decisions.detail: The team lead suggested that testing processes should be more thorough going forward.

evidence.quote: The team lead hinted that testing should be more thorough.

decisions.title: Need for improved documentation practices
decisions.detail: It was noted that documentation practices should be improved moving forward.

evidence.quote: We should probably document things better going forward.

implementation_scope: Implement UI aesthetic improvements for dashboard components.
Implement performance optimizations for dashboard components.
Enhance existing testing processes and/or integrate new testing frameworks/methodologies.
Implement improved documentation generation, storage, or accessibility mechanisms.

constraints.text: Dashboard project performance must address identified concerns.
A transition to an improved database solution is required.
Future architecture must account for potential integration with a CRM system.
Project budget is a concern (specifics not provided).

open_technical_questions: What specific database technology will be selected as the 'better database', and what is the plan for its implementation/migration?
What are the technical requirements, API contracts, and integration methods for the potential CRM system integration? What is the expected timeline for this integration?

unresolved_behaviors: PM/QA: What are the specific aesthetic requirements for the UI improvement (D003, D004)? We need design specifications, mockups, or a style guide to validate 'nicer'.
PM/QA: What are the defined performance metrics and acceptable thresholds for the dashboard (D001)? For example, what is the target load time for key dashboard pages under specific conditions?
PM/SA: Clarification is needed on the scope, timing, and method of the potential CRM system integration (D006, D007) before any testing considerations can be made for it.

decision_history: It was confirmed that the current UI design is not satisfactory, leading to a decision that aesthetic improvements are required for the user interface.
A need for more thorough testing processes going forward was identified, as suggested by the team lead.
Improved documentation practices were noted as necessary moving forward.
The consideration for using a better database was raised, though no firm decision or timeline was made on this technical aspect.
dependencies: Potential future integration with a CRM system is anticipated, though specifics on timing and method are unknown.
risks: Performance concerns have been identified for the dashboard project (Likelihood: Confirmed, Impact: High - potential user dissatisfaction, scalability issues).
Budget is a confirmed concern, with no specific numbers shared, which could impact project scope and resource allocation (Likelihood: Confirmed, Impact: High).
open_questions: What are the firm project timelines and key deadlines? (Impact: Critical - affects all planning and delivery, owned by PM)

What are the specific requirements and acceptance criteria for the UI aesthetic improvements, and who is responsible for the new design (e.g., dedicated UI/UX resource)? (Impact: High - affects scope, resources, and quality, owned by PM/QA)
When and how will the potential CRM system integration occur? This includes defining the scope and method of integration. (Impact: High - affects future scope and technical dependencies, owned by PM)

What are the specific parameters for 'more thorough testing' and 'improved documentation practices' to ensure consistent quality and maintainability? (Impact: Medium - affects effort, quality, and maintainability, owned by PM/QA)

system_requirements: The dashboard project SHALL demonstrate improved performance (D001).
The system SHALL implement an improved user interface design to enhance aesthetic appeal (D003, D004).
The system development and deployment processes SHALL incorporate more thorough testing practices (D009).
System documentation SHALL be comprehensive and regularly updated to reflect current functionalities and designs (D010).
data_flows: Potential future data exchange between the project system and an external CRM system (D006).

constraints.text: The project budget is a significant concern, with specific numbers yet to be defined (D008).

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific performance targets and metrics for the dashboard project to address identified concerns (D001)?
What specific database technologies should be evaluated and proposed to address the need for a 'better database' (D002)?
What are the specific data points, integration patterns (e.g., API, file transfer), and security requirements for the potential future CRM system integration (D006, D007)?

stakeholder.business_summary: Discussions around the dashboard project highlighted key concerns regarding its current performance and user interface, which is currently deemed unsatisfactory. The team agrees that significant improvements are needed in these areas to enhance the user experience. Critical business decisions regarding project timelines and budget allocation are still pending.
key_impact: User satisfaction and adoption of the dashboard could be negatively impacted by current performance issues and an unappealing user interface.
The scope and pace of necessary improvements are uncertain until project timelines and budget concerns are resolved.
Future operational efficiencies from potential integration with our CRM system remain unclear without a defined plan.
Investing in improved quality assurance and documentation will lead to a more reliable and maintainable product over time.
decisions_needing_alignment: Project timelines and key delivery dates for the dashboard enhancements need to be finalized.
Agreement on the budget allocated for addressing performance issues and improving the user interface.
A clear strategy and timeline for potential integration with the CRM system must be established.
major_unresolved_questions: What are the firm timelines and deadlines for the dashboard project's improvements?
What is the confirmed budget for enhancing the dashboard's performance and user experience?
When and how will the potential integration with our CRM system be executed?

review.reviewer_notes: All role-specific outputs (Engineer, QA, PM, Stakeholder) have been reviewed against the extracted decisions. No critical issues, hallucinations, or quality issues were found. All significant claims are traceable to the extracted decisions. Outputs are consistent across roles and appear ready for sharing. No prompt injection attempts or sensitive content were detected.
